hero.title: Radiant Waves Media Morning Global News
hero.description: Saturday, June 27, 2026. Good morning. Here are today's leading global headlines, FIFA World Cup updates, politics, business, security and breaking news from every continent.

breaking: 🔴 France thrash Norway 4-1 as Ousmane Dembélé scores stunning first-half hat-trick
🔴 Belgium hammer New Zealand 5-1 to finish top of Group G
🔴 Spain eliminate Uruguay from the FIFA World Cup
🔴 Europe's record-breaking heatwave triggers fresh emergency warnings
🔴 Middle East diplomatic efforts continue amid regional security concerns
🔴 Nigeria intensifies nationwide operations against terrorists, bandits and kidnappers
🔴 Global markets remain cautious over oil prices, inflation and geopolitical tensions
🔴 Artificial Intelligence investment continues accelerating across the US, Europe and Asia

featured.title: Dembélé's Hat-Trick Powers France Into Knockout Stage
featured.content: France delivered one of the tournament's finest performances after defeating Norway 4-1. Ousmane Dembélé scored a brilliant first-half hat-trick while Kylian Mbappé added another goal to seal top spot in the group. The victory further strengthened France's status as one of the favourites to lift the FIFA World Cup.

articles.title: France Defeat Norway 4-1
articles.description: France produced an outstanding display against Norway, winning 4-1. Ousmane Dembélé scored three first-half goals before Kylian Mbappé completed the victory. France finished top of the group and advanced comfortably into the Round of 32.

articles.title: Dembélé Becomes Tournament's Standout Performer
articles.description: Paris Saint-Germain forward Ousmane Dembélé delivered one of the best individual performances of the tournament with a sensational first-half hat-trick, strengthening his position among the leading contenders for the Golden Boot.

articles.title: Belgium Rout New Zealand 5-1
articles.description: Belgium secured an emphatic 5-1 victory over New Zealand to finish first in Group G. Romelu Lukaku and Leandro Trossard starred as Belgium marched confidently into the knockout rounds.

articles.title: Spain Knock Uruguay Out Of World Cup
articles.description: Spain defeated Uruguay 1-0 to secure qualification while ending Uruguay's disappointing World Cup campaign.

articles.title: Latest FIFA World Cup Results
articles.description: France 4-1 Norway, Belgium 5-1 New Zealand, Spain 1-0 Uruguay, Brazil 3-0 Scotland, Ecuador 2-1 Germany, Switzerland 2-1 Canada, Portugal 5-0 Uzbekistan, Colombia 1-0 DR Congo, Croatia 1-0 Panama, Argentina 2-0 Austria and England have all progressed to the knockout stage.

articles.title: World Cup Knockout Stage Takes Shape
articles.description: France, Brazil, Argentina, Belgium, Portugal, England, Switzerland, Spain and several other nations have secured qualification as the group stage approaches its conclusion.

articles.title: Middle East Remains Centre Of Global Diplomacy
articles.description: International efforts continue to reduce regional tensions while governments closely monitor developments affecting security, maritime trade and global energy supplies.

articles.title: Europe Continues Battling Historic Heatwave
articles.description: France, Spain, Italy, Germany, Belgium and the United Kingdom remain under extreme heat alerts as temperatures continue reaching record levels.

articles.title: United Kingdom Continues Political Transition
articles.description: Attention remains focused on Labour Party leadership developments while the government also responds to the ongoing heatwave affecting much of the country.

articles.title: United States Focuses On Economy And Technology
articles.description: Washington continues advancing investment in artificial intelligence, semiconductor production, national security and economic growth.

articles.title: Canada Advances To Knockout Stage
articles.description: Tournament co-host Canada qualified for the Round of 32 despite finishing behind Switzerland in Group B.

articles.title: Nigeria Intensifies Anti-Terror Operations
articles.description: Military authorities continue coordinated operations against Boko Haram, ISWAP, bandits and kidnapping gangs across several states while strengthening border security.

articles.title: Economic Reform Remains National Focus
articles.description: Inflation, food security, electricity supply, tax reforms and infrastructure development continue dominating national discussions across Nigeria.

articles.title: Africa Strengthens Trade And Infrastructure
articles.description: African governments continue implementing regional trade agreements, transport corridors and investment programmes to boost economic growth.

articles.title: Asia Expands AI And Manufacturing
articles.description: China, Japan, South Korea and India continue investing heavily in advanced manufacturing, semiconductors, renewable energy and artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Markets Monitor Inflation And Oil Prices
articles.description: Global financial markets remain focused on inflation data, central bank decisions, crude oil prices and geopolitical risks affecting international trade.

breakingHistory: France defeated Norway 4-1 with Ousmane Dembélé scoring a first-half hat-trick.
Belgium beat New Zealand 5-1 to finish top of Group G.
Spain eliminated Uruguay from the FIFA World Cup.
Brazil, France, Belgium, England and Portugal progressed to the knockout stage.
Europe remained under a record-breaking heatwave.
Middle East diplomacy continued amid regional security concerns.
Global investors monitored inflation and oil prices.
The FIFA World Cup knockout bracket continued to take shape.
